behavior_summary: Provides scene/event logic for a daemon/Pyros encounter and a small per-item dispatcher. The module contains a main event handler that, when invoked with a subject object and a list, ignores NPC subjects and otherwise iterates the list to dispatch an oriented action for each element using the subject's facing.
Includes a short helper that makes an object emit agonized vocal lines with small waits between lines (hurt/dying vocalization).

Includes a large orchestrator that sequences a multi-step encounter: playing music, branching dialog by a global flag (acolyteKilled), creating/destroying map items and NPCs, issuing orientation/path commands, casting multiple fire-spell effects, making NPCs speak, and coordinating timing with spawn/suspend/delays.
notable_control_flow: U8P_DAEMSCEN::0080 immediately sets object info then checks Item::isNpc(object); if true it returns early (guard against running on NPCs).

If the subject is not an NPC, U8P_DAEMSCEN::0080 iterates the provided item_list (foreach). For each element it reads the subject's facing via Npc::getDir and then spawns METHOD::15E7 for that list element with the facing as an argument; each spawn is awaited (implies/suspend semantics) so the routine dispatches actions sequentially.

U8P_DAEMSCEN::014B branches behavior and dialogue based on the global flag acolyteKilled and sequences many timed spawns/destroys/creates and spell casts; it uses short waits (FREE::1C19) and speech spawns (METHOD::087D) to pace the scene.
U8P_DAEMSCEN::00D1 performs short vocalization steps by spawning speech METHOD::087D and short delay FREE::1C19 between lines.
open_questions: Exact in-game effect of METHOD::15E7 (animation, attack, movement, or effect) when spawned with a direction byte — inferred but not explicit.
Precise semantics and meaning of the object info keys set by the scripts (e.g., the 0x210-like info referenced in related scripts) and any side-effects on AI/process exclusion.
Which external functions call U8P_DAEMSCEN::0080 in the engine (event id or trigger type — area entry, script event, or item activation) is not explicit.
Full parameter ordering for some spawned methods (some calls use pid + text + byte, others push item pointers first) — ordering was inferred from usage patterns.

main_function.name: U8P_DAEMSCEN::0080
main_function.purpose: Event handler that, when given a process id, a subject object and a list of targets, ignores NPC subjects and otherwise dispatches a per-list-element, direction-oriented action by reading the subject's facing and spawning METHOD::15E7 for each element (with suspend/await between spawns).
entry_conditions: Called with pid (process id), object_ptr (subject) and item_list (list of targets).
Intended to run only when the passed object is not an NPC (it checks Item::isNpc and returns early on true).

function_map.name: U8P_DAEMSCEN::0080
function_map.role: Per-list dispatcher: gate by non-NPC subject and spawn oriented actions (METHOD::15E7) for each list element.

function_map.name: U8P_DAEMSCEN::00D1
function_map.role: Short vocalization routine: set info on an object and spawn speech lines with short waits (agonized/cry vocalization).

function_map.name: U8P_DAEMSCEN::014B
function_map.role: Large encounter/scene orchestrator for a Pyros/daemon event: plays music, chooses dialogue by acolyteKilled, spawns/destroys items and NPCs, issues pathfinding and orientation, casts multiple fire-spell effects, and sequences timed speech/actions.

function_map.name: Item::isNpc
function_map.role: Engine query to decide whether an item pointer refers to an NPC (used as an early exit guard).

function_map.name: Npc::getDir
function_map.role: Engine helper returning the facing/direction of an NPC/object (used to orient spawned effects/actions).

function_map.name: METHOD::15E7
function_map.role: Spawned action applied to list elements with a direction argument; likely an oriented effect/animation/attack (inferred).

internal_calls.purpose: Likely used to produce agonized vocal lines for created NPCs/objects during the scene (inferred from similar speech/delay patterns in 00D1).

external_calls.purpose: Guard: check whether the event subject is an NPC and early-return if true.
Read the subject's facing/direction to parameterize per-target spawned actions.
Spawn the per-list-element action/behavior using the previously obtained direction; spawns are awaited/suspended to sequence them.
Spawn speech/dialogue lines (make an agent 'bark' a scream/line).
Short timed wait/delay between speech lines to pace vocalization.
Make created NPCs or the encounter emit dialogue/barks at staged times.
Pop new items or NPCs into the scene at computed coordinates as part of encounter setup.
Read coordinates of scene anchors or the player/objects for distance checks and spawn placement.
Place temporary map tiles/anchors then remove or trigger them as part of the scene sequencing.
Compute directional targeting between items/NPCs before spawning oriented effects.
Spawn/execute multiple fire-spell effects at locations or on targets during the encounter.
Initialize newly created NPCs (appearance/state) with a small byte argument after creation.
Trigger a special encounter effect (teleport/pentagram) on list items with pid; used as part of scene mechanics.

reads.name: acolyteKilled
reads.likely_effect: Selects dialogue variants and named targets used by U8P_DAEMSCEN::014B (changes who speaks/which lines and some scene branching).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides scene/event logic for a daemon/Pyros encounter and a small per-item dispatcher. The module contains a main event handler that, when invoked with a subject object and a list, ignores NPC subjects and otherwise iterates the list to dispatch an oriented action for each element using the subject's facing.
